explanation: This command uses standard PowerShell syntax to download and execute a script via IEX, which is directly detected by the Sigma rule. It serves as a baseline for testing detection accuracy.
This command uses double quotes around the entire command line, which is a safe insertion technique. The Sigma rule still detects it because it contains the IEX keyword in the expected context.
This command omits the surrounding quotes, which is a safe omission technique. The Sigma rule still detects it because the IEX keyword is present in the command line.
This command uses the lowercase version of the IEX alias, which is a substitution technique. The Sigma rule is case-insensitive and still detects it.
This command uses parentheses around the IEX argument, which is a safe reordering technique. The Sigma rule still detects it because the IEX keyword is present.
